text: {"analysis_summary": "The inspected RTL includes externally exposed JTAG/debug logic and debug bus masters. The visible source shows JTAG pads entering the top-level design, being routed through safe_domain into jtag_tap_top, and selecting an AXI/LINT debug scan path via axireg_sel_o. The selected debug path is connected in pulp_soc to lint_jtag_wrap/adbg_lintonly_top, which drives a master port onto s_lint_debug_bus. Separately, the adv_dbg_if IP contains an advanced debug TAP and adbg_top/adbg_axi_module/adbg_axi_biu path capable of issuing AXI read/write transactions. Across the visible files, I did not find any real permission/authentication/lock/secure-state gating for these debug accesses. A comment-only fake PASSWORD line exists but is not logic. Based on the visible evidence, the code contains permission-related vulnerabilities: debug/JTAG access appears to be enabled by JTAG instruction selection alone and can generate bus transactions without authorization checks.", "findings": [{"finding_id": "PERM-JTAG-LINT-UNAUTH-001", "status": "confirmed_finding", "summary": "Externally accessible JTAG path can select and drive an internal debug bus master without visible authorization gating.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing authorization / improper debug permission control", "affected_locations": [{"file": "line_start": 453, "line_end": 474, "module": "safe_domain", "signal_or_register": "jtag_tap_top_i / axireg_sel_o"}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 12, "line_end": 42, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "signal_or_register": "JTAG inputs, axireg_sel_o, dbg_axi_scan_in_o/dbg_axi_scan_out_i"}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 57, "line_end": 84, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "signal_or_register": "tap_top_i .axireg_sel_o / scan path"}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 126, "line_end": 130, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "signal_or_register": "dbg_axi_scan_in_o, soc_jtag_reg_o, sel_fll_clk_o"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/pulp_soc.sv", "line_start": 351, "line_end": 351, "module": "pulp_soc", "signal_or_register": "s_lint_debug_bus"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/pulp_soc.sv", "line_start": 737, "line_end": 749, "module": "pulp_soc", "signal_or_register": "i_lint_jtag / jtag_lint_master"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/lint_jtag_wrap.sv", "line_start": 14, "line_end": 29, "module": "lint_jtag_wrap", "signal_or_register": "lint_select_i, jtag_lint_master"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/lint_jtag_wrap.sv", "line_start": 35, "line_end": 66, "module": "lint_jtag_wrap", "signal_or_register": "adbg_lintonly_top outputs lint_req_o/lint_add_o/lint_wen_o/lint_wdata_o"}], "evidence": [{"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/pulpissimo.sv", "line_start": 81, "line_end": 85, "module": "pulpissimo", "object": "pad_jtag_*", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "Top-level module exposes physical/inout JTAG pads as external ports.", "supports_claim": "External JTAG access is present at chip/top-level boundary."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/pulpissimo.sv", "line_start": 539, "line_end": 543, "module": "pulpissimo", "object": "pad_jtag_* instance connections", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "Search results show the top-level JTAG pads are connected inside pulpissimo.", "supports_claim": "External JTAG pins are integrated into the design rather than left unused."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/safe_domain.sv", "line_start": 38, "line_end": 47, "module": "safe_domain", "object": "soc_tck_o/soc_trstn_o/soc_tms_o/soc_tdi_o/axireg_sel_o/axireg_tdi_o", "evidence_type": "source/search_result", "description": "safe_domain declares JTAG inputs and outputs SoC JTAG/debug scan signals including axireg_sel_o and axireg_tdi/tdo signals.", "supports_claim": "The safe domain forwards and exposes JTAG/debug selection signals toward the SoC/debug path."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/safe_domain.sv", "line_start": 453, "line_end": 474, "module": "safe_domain", "object": "jtag_tap_top_i", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "safe_domain instantiates jtag_tap_top and connects axireg_sel_o from that TAP instance.", "supports_claim": "The JTAG TAP drives the AXI/debug-register select signal used downstream."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 12, "line_end": 42, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "object": "module ports", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "jtag_tap_top has JTAG input ports and debug scan interface ports, including axireg_sel_o and dbg_axi_scan_in_o/dbg_axi_scan_out_i.", "supports_claim": "The TAP directly bridges external JTAG operations to a debug scan interface."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 57, "line_end": 84, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "object": "tap_top_i", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "jtag_tap_top instantiates tap_top and wires .axireg_sel_o(axireg_sel_o), .scan_in_o(s_scan_i), and .axireg_out_i(dbg_axi_scan_out_i). No authorization/lock input is present in this visible connection.", "supports_claim": "Debug AXI register selection is controlled by TAP instruction/scan logic with no visible permission gate."}, {"file": "rtl/pulpissimo/jtag_tap_top.sv", "line_start": 126, "line_end": 126, "module": "jtag_tap_top", "object": "dbg_axi_scan_in_o", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "jtag_tap_top assigns dbg_axi_scan_in_o = s_scan_i, making the TAP scan data available to the debug path.", "supports_claim": "JTAG scan input is passed directly into the debug AXI scan path."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/pulp_soc.sv", "line_start": 351, "line_end": 351, "module": "pulp_soc", "object": "s_lint_debug_bus", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "pulp_soc declares a debug/local-interconnect bus s_lint_debug_bus.", "supports_claim": "A dedicated debug bus exists inside the SoC."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/pulp_soc.sv", "line_start": 737, "line_end": 749, "module": "pulp_soc", "object": "i_lint_jtag", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "pulp_soc connects lint_jtag_wrap with .lint_select_i(jtag_axireg_sel_i) and .jtag_lint_master(s_lint_debug_bus).", "supports_claim": "The TAP-selected debug path becomes a bus master on s_lint_debug_bus."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/lint_jtag_wrap.sv", "line_start": 14, "line_end": 29, "module": "lint_jtag_wrap", "object": "module ports", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "lint_jtag_wrap module ports include lint_select_i and XBAR_TCDM_BUS.Master jtag_lint_master.", "supports_claim": "The JTAG wrapper is designed to act as a master interface to the debug/local bus."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/lint_jtag_wrap.sv", "line_start": 35, "line_end": 66, "module": "lint_jtag_wrap", "object": "dbg_module_i", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "lint_jtag_wrap instantiates adbg_lintonly_top, feeding .debug_select_i(lint_select_i) and driving jtag_lint_master.req/add/wen/wdata/be from lint_req_o/lint_add_o/lint_wen_o/lint_wdata_o/lint_be_o.", "supports_claim": "JTAG-selected debug logic can generate local bus requests and writes. No visible permission check mediates those outputs."}, {"file": ".", "line_start": null, "line_end": null, "module": "multiple", "object": "auth/permission/secure/PASSWORD searches", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "Searches for permission/security terms found no substantive authorization logic; only license text for permission and a comment-only fake PASSWORD line in adv_dbg_if.sv.", "supports_claim": "The visible source lacks evidence of real authentication or permission gating for debug/JTAG access."}], "reasoning_summary": "The design exposes JTAG pads, routes them to a TAP, uses TAP instruction selection to assert axireg_sel_o, and connects that selection to lint_jtag_wrap. lint_jtag_wrap then instantiates adbg_lintonly_top, whose outputs directly drive a master bus interface. The visible control condition is selection by JTAG/TAP state, not a privilege, fuse, lifecycle, authenticated unlock, or secure-mode policy. Therefore any actor with JTAG access and knowledge of the protocol could potentially issue local debug bus reads/writes.", "security_impact": "Unauthorized JTAG users may gain read/write access to internal SoC memory or control/status registers through the local debug bus. This can enable privilege bypass, extraction of secrets, modification of firmware/data, disabling of security controls, or persistent compromise during production use.", "confidence": "high", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The analysis was limited to visible files and tool output. Some instantiated modules such as tap_top, adbg_lintonly_top internals, interconnect address maps, lifecycle controller, or pad-frame details may be outside the visible/truncated evidence. However, searches over the visible scope did not reveal substantive auth/secure/permission logic, and the shown integration provides no authorization input on the critical debug-select path.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Add an explicit debug authorization mechanism, such as lifecycle/fuse-gated debug enable plus authenticated challenge-response unlock.", "Gate axireg_sel_o/debug_select_i and all debug bus request outputs with a synchronized, tamper-resistant debug_allowed signal.", "Ensure production reset defaults disable JTAG debug access and only allow non-invasive boundary scan if required.", "Add address-range filtering for debug masters so JTAG cannot access secret/key/OTP/security-control regions even when debug is enabled.", "Verify with formal or simulation properties that jtag_lint_master.req and write enables cannot assert unless debug authorization is active."]}, {"finding_id": "PERM-ADBG-AXI-UNAUTH-002", "status": "potential_warning", "summary": "Advanced debug IP can generate AXI master transactions from JTAG-selected debug commands without visible permission checks.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing authorization for debug-to-AXI master access", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 21, "line_end": 40, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "signal_or_register": "tms_pad_i/tck_pad_i/trstn_pad_i/tdi_pad_i/tdo_pad_o"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 117, "line_end": 162, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "signal_or_register": "cluster_tap_i / s_debug_select / s_debug_tdo"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 159, "line_end": 172, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "signal_or_register": "dbg_module_i .debug_select_i(s_debug_select)"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 149, "line_end": 149, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "module_selects"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 191, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "debug_select_i command/shift handling"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 201, "line_end": 218, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "adbg_axi_module module_select_i(module_selects[0])"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_module.sv", "line_start": 87, "line_end": 87, "module": "adbg_axi_module", "signal_or_register": "module_select_i"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_module.sv", "line_start": 469, "line_end": 482, "module": "adbg_axi_module", "signal_or_register": "adbg_axi_biu .strobe_i(biu_strobe)"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_module.sv", "line_start": 718, "line_end": 880, "module": "adbg_axi_module", "signal_or_register": "biu_strobe"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 91, "line_end": 140, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "signal_or_register": "AXI master outputs"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 530, "line_end": 532, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "signal_or_register": "axi_master_aw_valid / axi_master_ar_valid"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 21, "line_end": 40, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "JTAG pads and AXI master ports", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "adv_dbg_if exposes raw JTAG pad signals and an AXI master interface as module ports.", "supports_claim": "The IP is externally controllable by JTAG and can issue AXI transactions."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 117, "line_end": 162, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "cluster_tap_i", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "adv_dbg_if instantiates adbg_tap_top, producing s_debug_select from JTAG TAP instruction selection and feeding debug_tdo back to the TAP.", "supports_claim": "JTAG selection controls the advanced debug module path."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 159, "line_end": 172, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "dbg_module_i", "evidence_type": "source/search_result", "description": "adv_dbg_if instantiates adbg_top and connects .debug_select_i(s_debug_select).", "supports_claim": "The JTAG TAP debug selection directly enables the debug module."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 149, "line_end": 178, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "module_selects", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "adbg_top has a module_selects register for individual debug modules and visible search results show assignments selecting AXI and CPU modules at lines 176 and 178.", "supports_claim": "JTAG commands can select debug submodules; no separate permission state is visible."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 191, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "debug_select_i && select_cmd / shift_dr_i", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "adbg_top updates module selection and shift register behavior when debug_select_i is asserted with update_dr_i or shift_dr_i.", "supports_claim": "The control gate is TAP debug selection, not authentication."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 201, "line_end": 218, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "adbg_axi_module", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "adbg_top instantiates adbg_axi_module and connects module_select_i to module_selects[0].", "supports_claim": "Selected JTAG debug commands can activate the AXI debug module."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_module.sv", "line_start": 87, "line_end": 744, "module": "adbg_axi_module", "object": "module_select_i conditions", "evidence_type": "search_result/source", "description": "adbg_axi_module exposes module_select_i and contains many state transitions/conditions controlled by module_select_i, capture/update/shift, and module command signals.", "supports_claim": "AXI debug operations are driven by selected JTAG module state."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_module.sv", "line_start": 469, "line_end": 880, "module": "adbg_axi_module", "object": "biu_strobe / adbg_axi_biu", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "adbg_axi_module instantiates adbg_axi_biu and connects .strobe_i(biu_strobe). Search results show biu_strobe asserted at multiple operation states.", "supports_claim": "JTAG debug commands can start BIU transactions."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 91, "line_end": 140, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "AXI master port list", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "adbg_axi_biu has AXI master read/write address/data/control outputs, including aw_valid, aw_addr, ar_valid, ar_addr, w_valid, and w_data.", "supports_claim": "The debug BIU can act as an AXI master capable of reading/writing system address space."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 381, "line_end": 532, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "axi_master_aw_valid/axi_master_ar_valid/axi_master_aw_addr", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "Search results show adbg_axi_biu drives axi_master_aw_valid = 1'b1 and axi_master_ar_valid = 1'b1, and assigns axi_master_aw_addr = addr_reg.", "supports_claim": "The BIU actively issues AXI bus requests to addresses supplied through the debug path."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 11, "line_end": 13, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "PASSWORD comments", "evidence_type": "source", "description": "adv_dbg_if contains only comment text mentioning PASSWORD, including '//PASSWORD = 0x1c10_0001; //but not really'; no real password signal or comparison was visible.", "supports_claim": "Apparent password references are non-functional comments and do not provide authorization."}], "reasoning_summary": "The advanced debug interface IP routes JTAG TAP selection to adbg_top. adbg_top selects submodules including an AXI debug module. The AXI debug module generates biu_strobe operations into adbg_axi_biu, and the BIU exposes/defines AXI master transactions. The visible source uses debug_select_i/module_select_i and JTAG state as the enabling conditions; no authentication, privilege, lock, fuse, or lifecycle enable was found in the relevant path. This creates an unauthorized debug master risk if the IP is integrated in a production design or reachable through pads.", "security_impact": "If this IP is reachable in the integrated SoC, a JTAG attacker can potentially issue arbitrary AXI reads/writes, inspect or modify memory and peripherals, control CPU debug signals such as stall/reset, and bypass software privilege checks or secure boot assumptions.", "confidence": "medium", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "Search results in rtl/pulpissimo did not show adv_dbg_if instantiated there, so this may be an included IP not used by the top-level visible integration. The vulnerability is confirmed within the IP itself but its exploitability depends on integration. Some file contents were truncated, but searches over visible scope did not reveal real auth/secure gating.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Do not include or connect adv_dbg_if in production unless protected by lifecycle/fuse-authenticated debug enable.", "Add a mandatory authorization input to adv_dbg_if/adbg_top/adbg_axi_module/adbg_axi_biu and gate module selection, biu_strobe, AXI valid, CPU stall/reset, and CPU debug writes with it.", "Restrict the AXI debug master through an address firewall or interconnect policy that blocks security-sensitive regions by default.", "Remove misleading password comments or replace them with actual documented secure debug-unlock logic if required.", "Add assertions that axi_master_aw_valid/ar_valid/w_valid cannot assert when debug authorization is false."]}], "no_finding_reason": "", "global_uncertainty": "The tool outputs were sometimes truncated, and not all instantiated module definitions were visible in full. The analysis is limited to files under the provided scope and visible tool results. Integration-specific lifecycle/debug policy, pad mux restrictions, or security firewalls may exist outside the inspected/visible evidence. Nevertheless, within the visible RTL, critical debug paths lack explicit authorization signals or permission checks."}
